fresh_facts.claim: The Pentagon ordered approximately 2,200-2,500 Marines to deploy to the Middle East, including elements of the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit and the USS Tripoli amphibious assault ship.
President Trump announced that U.S. Central Command executed bombing raids on Iran's Kharg Island, claiming the operation 'totally obliterated every MILITARY target' at this location.
Israel announced that strikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon are 'continuing and intensifying.'
Iran conducted missile and drone strikes targeting military installations and commercial vessels across the region near shipping routes linked to the Strait of Hormuz.
A missile strike hit the helipad inside the U.S. Embassy compound in Baghdad, with video obtained by The Associated Press showing smoke billowing from inside the facility.
Drone strikes in northern Iraq killed a French soldier and wounded several others stationed there as part of an international coalition.
The Strait of Hormuz, which carries roughly 20% of the world's oil supply and significant liquefied natural gas exports, became effectively closed due to Iranian missile and drone attacks.
Iranian security forces increased their presence in streets to prevent demonstrations while the government simultaneously encouraged supporters to gather in street demonstrations.
The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad renewed its Level 4 security alert for Iraq, warning that Iran and Iran-aligned militia groups 'may continue to target' U.S. citizens, interests, and infrastructure.
Mojtaba Khamenei was recently elevated to Supreme Leader following his father Ali Khamenei's death.
U.S. and Israeli intelligence assess that the IRGC is effectively making decisions due to Mojtaba Khamenei's injuries, portraying him as a weaker, more pliant figure.
Iranian security forces announced 500 arrests since March 14 for alleged espionage and anti-regime activities.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told CBS News on March 15 that Iran has not requested a ceasefire or U.S. talks and will defend itself 'as long as it takes.'
President Trump stated on March 14 to NBC News that Iran seeks a deal but its terms are insufficient, rejecting Middle East allies' mediation efforts.
The rapid appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei marks the first father-son power transfer of Supreme Leader authority since 1979.
Intelligence reports from March 15 indicate Mojtaba Khamenei is wounded, with the IRGC 'calling the shots' on military and political decisions.
PMOI Resistance Units endorsed the NCRI's Provisional Government on March 16, rejecting dictatorships amid wartime crackdowns.
Grassroots campaigns by PMOI Resistance Units defied security measures on March 16, promoting a democratic republic.
A PMOI commander reported clashes during a February 23 offensive on Khamenei's Tehran headquarters, with local resident support.
The UN Fact-Finding Mission warned on March 11 of escalating civilian risks from repression in Iran.
U.S. policy demands Iran cease terrorism, nuclear pursuits, and brutality; allied statements on March 16 urge no operational pauses to prevent regime rebuilding.
U.S. and Israeli strikes targeted IRGC/Basij sites in Hamedan on March 15.
Iranian media claimed a first Sejjil missile launch at Israel on March 15.
Wartime strikes on infrastructure occurred across six provinces since March 14.

regime_fall.current_status: Following Ali Khamenei's death and his son Mojtaba's succession on March 16, 2026—the first father-to-son Supreme Leader transfer since 1979—US-Israeli forces maintain intensive military operations across Iran while intelligence assessments indicate Mojtaba is wounded with the IRGC effectively controlling military and political decisions amid widespread internal security crackdowns and armed opposition activity.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mojtaba Khamenei was elevated to Supreme Leader following his father Ali Khamenei's death on March 16, 2026, marking the first father-to-son power transfer since the 1979 revolution, while US intelligence reports indicate he is wounded and the IRGC is effectively making decisions.
